invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
Commands that initiate data transfer (LIST, NLST, RETR, STOR, APPE, MLSD) can only be sent after a successful login (230 response to PASS).
A data connection setup command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must be issued before any data transfer command (LIST, RETR, etc.) in each transfer cycle.
The QUIT command is valid at any point during the control connection life cycle.
If a PASS command results in a 530 error, the client must restart the authentication sequence with a new USER command.
A RNTO (Rename To) command must be immediately preceded by a RNFR (Rename From) command.
CWD.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
RETR.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
STOR.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
DELE.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
RNFR.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
RNTO.path: Specifies path or filename for operation

ordering_rules: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
Commands that initiate data transfer (LIST, NLST, RETR, STOR, APPE, MLSD) can only be sent after a successful login (230 response to PASS).
A data connection setup command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must be issued before any data transfer command (LIST, RETR, etc.) in each transfer cycle.
The QUIT command is valid at any point during the control connection life cycle.
If a PASS command results in a 530 error, the client must restart the authentication sequence with a new USER command.
A RNTO (Rename To) command must be immediately preceded by a RNFR (Rename From) command.
field_constraints: USER.username: Format: USER <username>
PASS.password: Format: PASS <password>
PORT.address_port: Format: PORT h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2
TYPE.type_code: Must be 'A' (ASCII) or 'I' (Image/Binary)
CWD.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
RETR.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
STOR.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
DELE.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
RNFR.path: Specifies path or filename for operation
RNTO.path: Specifies path or filename for operation

status_changes.rationale: 331 response indicates the server received the username and is waiting for a password.
230 response confirms successful login.
530 response indicates failure, remaining in a pre-login state often allows retrying or restarting.
Possible if a channel is already established or default parameters are used.
REIN reinitializes the session back to the greeting state.
Sequence starts over after session reset.
221 response signifies connection termination.
Directory change is an atomic operation within the authenticated session.
Working directory query is an atomic operation within the authenticated session.
Account command stays within the authenticated session context.
Parent directory change stays within the authenticated session context.
Equivalent to CWD.
Equivalent to PWD.
Equivalent to CDUP.
Directory creation stays within session.
Equivalent to MKD.
Directory removal stays within session.
Equivalent to RMD.
File deletion stays within session.
Transfer type setting stays within session.
Transfer mode setting stays within session.
File structure setting stays within session.
Structure mount stays within session.
System type query stays within session.
Feature query stays within session.
Help query stays within session.
No-op stays within session.
Machine listing (single item) stays within session (sent on control channel).
File size query stays within session.
Status query stays within session.
EPSV is the extended version of PASV.
EPRT is the extended version of PORT.
Machine listing directory requires data channel. Direct transition implies implicit channel setup or state omission.
Retrieve command requires data channel. Direct transition implies implicit channel setup or state omission.
Store command requires data channel. Direct transition implies implicit channel setup or state omission.
Append command requires data channel. Direct transition implies implicit channel setup or state omission.
Directory listing initiated on ready channel.
Append initiated on ready channel.
Return to session state after transfer completion.
Return to session state after transfer completion.
Return to session state after transfer completion.
Return to session state after transfer completion.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
status_changes.rationale: Standard RFC 959 behavior for authentication sequence.

status_changes.description: A data connection setup command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must be issued before any data transfer command (LIST, RETR, etc.) in each transfer cycle.
status_changes.rationale: FTP's out-of-band data architecture requires a separate channel setup for each transfer operation.

status_changes.description: If a PASS command results in a 530 error, the client must restart the authentication sequence with a new USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Many servers reset the user context upon failure, necessitating a new USER command.

status_changes.description: A RNTO (Rename To) command must be immediately preceded by a RNFR (Rename From) command.
status_changes.rationale: RFC 959 specifies this command sequence for file renaming.
Standard field format.
Standard field format.

status_changes.rationale: Standard command parameter.
